records.ety: [OE. avis, F. avis; <?/ + OF. vis, fr. L. visum seemed, seen; really p. p. of videre to see, so that vis meant that which has seemed best. See Vision, and cf. Avise, Advise.]

defs: An opinion recommended or offered, as worthy to be followed; counsel.
quotes: We may give advice, but we can not give conduct.
How shall I dote on her with more advice, That thus without advice begin to love her?

defs: Information or notice given; intelligence; as, late advices from France; -- commonly in the plural.
notes: ☞ In commercial language, advice usually means information communicated by letter; -- used chiefly in reference to drafts or bills of exchange; as, a letter of advice.

defs: Counseling to perform a specific illegal act.
